title: Economic Bulletin
summary: This is a navigation/landing page for the ECB Economic Bulletin section, not an actual archived release. The page describes what the Economic Bulletin is (economic and monetary information released eight times yearly, two weeks after each ECB policy meeting) and provides links to download specific issues. No actual economic data, policy analysis, or market-relevant content is present in this source.
key_points: Page is a navigation listing for ECB Economic Bulletin issues, not the actual publication content
Economic Bulletin is described as presenting economic and monetary information for Governing Council policy decisions
Publication schedule: eight releases per year, two weeks after monetary policy meetings
March, June, September, and December issues include staff macroeconomic projections on inflation, growth, public finances, and external trade
Latest archived issue referenced is Issue 1, 2026 (published 19 February 2026)
No actual economic data, figures, or policy content is present on this page
why_it_matters: This page provides no actionable macro or market information. It is a structural metadata page describing the ECB Economic Bulletin publication framework. Researchers seeking ECB economic analysis should access the specific downloadable issues rather than this landing page. Per CORE RULE #6, listing pages are not archiveable.
